title: myosotis scorpioides subsp. praecox
intro: Myosotis scorpioides subsp. praecox, also known as creeping forget-me-not, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and acting as an antibacterial agent, making it useful in treating conditions like urinary tract infections and skin irritations. The plant can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and risks.
